---
title: "Survey says: Florida No. 2 in tech job growth"
date: "August 08, 2014"
---

# Survey says: Florida No. 2 in tech job growth

The survey says … Florida is No. 2 in tech job growth so far this year.

In just the first six months of 2014, Florida has created nearly as many tech jobs as the state did in all of last year, putting Florida in the No. 2 spot (behind Texas) in a [new report](http://pubads.g.doubleclick.net/pagead/adview?ai=BWMDo6fLhU6T4Bo_-ADqAMB4AQBkgULCAcQARgBIMGZ6BiSBQsIBxABGAEgmZ3qGJIFCwgHEAEYASDhweoYoAYg&sigh=HBbpt1Yf3Nw&adurl=http://pagead2.googlesyndication.com/pagead/imgad?id=CICAgKCNn-yIxgEQARgBMggPKtH7CIiUnA%26t%3D10%26cT%3Dhttp%253A//bizjournals.com%26l%3Dhttp%253A//media.dice.com/report/august-2014-dont-mess-with-tech/) from Dice, a career site for tech and engineering professionals. [according to Tampa Bay Business Journal](http://www.bizjournals.com/tampabay/news/2014/08/04/florida-ranks-no-2-in-tech-job-creation.html)[.](http://www.bizjournals.com/southflorida/blog/morning-edition/2014/08/florida-ranks-no-2-in-tech-job-creation.html)

Florida created 4,100 tech jobs from Jan. 1 through June 30, compared to 4,500 in all of 2013 -- a 5.6 percent growth rate. Also growing tech jobs quickly: North Carolina, Oregon and Washington.
